summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub, receiving repeated 502 error responses. This is a transient network failure, not a code defect.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated HTTP 502 errors when pip tried to fetch https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip, causing the connection pool to exhaust retries.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Explicit error message showing GitHub returned 502s until retries were exhausted, a transient server-side failure.
The test was automatically rerun (via pytest-rerunfailures), confirming the framework treated it as a flaky/transient failure, but it still failed after reruns.
The summary line shows 3 reruns, indicating the test was retried multiple times before being marked as a final failure, consistent with a flaky network issue.
The only test failure in the entire run is this network-dependent test targeting GitHub.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job; this is a transient GitHub 502 network failure. If it recurs, consider adding retry logic to the test or marking it with a network flakiness marker to skip or xfail under unreliable conditions.
